children.name: Brachypteracias
children.description: Ground rollers with relatively short legs and a more arboreal habit than their relatives. The Short-legged Ground Roller is the most forest-dependent of the ground rollers, preferring pristine humid rainforest.

children.name: Brachypteracias leptosomus
children.description: A striking ground roller endemic to the eastern rainforests of Madagascar, with a green back, blue wings, and a rufous breast. It has notably short legs and spends much of its time perched low in the understorey, sallying to the ground for prey. It is vulnerable to habitat loss as Madagascar's rainforests continue to shrink.

children.name: Geobiastes
children.description: A monotypic genus of ground roller with a heavily scaled appearance that provides excellent camouflage in the forest undergrowth. It is the most terrestrial of the ground rollers, spending much of its time on the forest floor.

children.name: Geobiastes squamiger
children.description: A secretive ground roller of lowland and mid-elevation rainforests in eastern Madagascar. Its plumage is a beautiful pattern of green, brown and black with white-tipped feathers that create a scaled effect. It is the most poorly known ground roller, rarely seen due to its shyness and cryptically patterned plumage.

children.name: Uratelornis
children.description: A monotypic genus of ground roller with an exceptionally long tail and a preference for the arid spiny forests of southern Madagascar. It is the largest and most striking ground roller.

children.name: Uratelornis chimaera
children.description: Endemic to the spiny and dry deciduous forests of southwestern Madagascar, this spectacular bird has a blue crown, rufous back, black throat, and extremely long graduated tail. It is one of Madagascar's most sought-after birds by visiting birdwatchers. It is vulnerable due to the ongoing degradation of its limited habitat.

children.name: Atelornis
children.description: Smaller, more colourful ground rollers that resemble pittas in both appearance and behaviour. They are among the most vividly plumaged of the ground rollers, with startling combinations of blue, green, rufous, and white.

children.name: Atelornis pittoides
children.description: A beautiful ground roller of Madagascar's eastern rainforests, with green upperparts, a blue crown and wings, and a rufous-orange breast. As its name suggests, it resembles a pitta in size, shape, and foraging behaviour, hopping on the forest floor in search of invertebrates. It is more common and visible than other ground roller species.
